Wiki / Blending & specials

Blending & special outcomes

Blending is the heart of the game. Here's the full loop — manual and automatic — plus the special outcomes that stack, blend speed, and how each shake's value is calculated.

The loop

How blending works

Manual blend

Load one or more flavors into the blender and click to blend. A blend takes 7.5 s by default, and each completed blend pays out money + XP immediately. Manual blends carry a small bonus over automated ones.

Auto Blend

Flip on Auto Blend with the button in the top-left corner to keep blending hands-free. Speed upgrades apply to both auto and manual blending, so it scales right alongside you.

Multiple blenders

You start with one blender. The Extra Blender special upgrade adds up to 3 more (4 total), each producing a shake per cycle for parallel output.

Passive income

Employees and locations earn money every second on top of blending. Turn on the $/s readout in Settings to track your rate — it's the single best number to optimize.

Specials

Special outcomes

Every blend can randomly come out "special" for a bonus — and outcomes stack on a single shake.

OutcomeApprox. chanceBonus
Crusty~10%+$10
Baked~5%+$25
Swirled~1%+$100
Golden~0.1%+$1,000

On top of those, blends can also roll quality, texture, and presentation results — Fan Favorite, Creamy, and Decorated. These feed your customer rating and stats rather than a fixed cash bonus.

Because outcomes stack independently, the jackpot shake is a Fan Favorite Creamy Crusty Baked Swirled Golden Decorated Blend — astronomically rare, and a badge of honor.

Tuning

Boosting your odds & speed

Better odds

  • Golden Touch — golden chance ×5 (one-time upgrade).
  • Special Batches (research) — +5–15% special chances.
  • Golden Formula (research) — +2–6% golden chance.
  • Fan Favorite Push (research) — +4–12% fan-favorite rate.

Blend speed

Base7.5 s
Mix Speed−0.2 s / level
Speed Blendinghalves time (one-time)
Blend Optimization−8% / −15% / −22%
Floor0.5 s

Bigger payouts

Portion Size and Yield Boost research raise the money per blend, while Double Shot doubles manual-blend income outright. Pair speed and yield for the best clicks-per-dollar.

Under the hood

How income is calculated

Each shake's value is a base amount scaled by a stack of multipliers. The global multiplier is the big lever.

The global multiplier

Multiplies all income. It combines:

  • Countries — product of all unlocked country multipliers (with diminishing returns).
  • Active flavors — the sum of your loaded flavors' multipliers.
  • Flavor combos — flat bonuses when the right flavors are active together.
  • Recipe Development, ingredient quality, storefront appeal, social-media buzz, and master-mixologist upgrades.

Manual vs. passive

Manual blends multiply the base shake value, your active flavors, portion size, and the global multiplier. Passive income sums your shops + locations, applies marketing/training/management upgrades, then the global multiplier — with a small automation factor so a hand-pulled shake is always worth a touch more.

Most upgrades feed one of these multipliers. When deciding what to buy, ask which term it boosts and how much — a flat global multiplier upgrade often beats a niche one. See Tips & strategy.

Flavors & combosWhat to load in the blender Shops & upgradesWhere the multipliers come from
